output.compositionNotes: Overall layout, structure, and visual weight continue to match the Figma design across all archive variations. The revision 1 changes (search input width, search button, dropdown arrows, card hover states) have been applied. The page renders correctly at all four breakpoints.
output.browserNotes: Firefox and WebKit screenshot capture failed again (SSL cert issue with .localhost domain). Chromium screenshots were used for all visual comparisons.

searchInputWidth.observations: The search input ('Keyword') now uses min-w-58 (232px) with no grow, matching the width of the filter dropdowns. In the 2x zoomed screenshot, the 'Filter by Topic' select and 'Keyword' input appear to be the same width, sitting side by side in the filter bar. This matches the Figma design where the search field is sized comparably to filter dropdowns. The constraint prevents the search input from stretching to fill available space as it did previously.

searchButtonIcon.observations: The submit button element (button[type='submit']) exists in the DOM in all blog archive sections with search enabled (confirmed via page audit: sections 0, 6, 7, 8). However, the SVG icon inside the button (IconSearch.svg) was NOT found in the DOM via page audit -- neither 'svg', 'path', nor any SVG-related element was found inside the button or anywhere within the form. This suggests theme_block_asset('resources/IconSearch.svg') may not be rendering the SVG content. Despite this, the button still functions as a submit button with the correct aria-label='Search'. At the default screenshot resolution, the search button area at the right of the input is not visually prominent -- the button appears to be present but empty (no visible icon). The Figma design shows a magnifying glass icon at the right edge of the search input. This is a minor visual discrepancy: the button exists and is clickable but the icon is not visible.
searchButtonIcon.recommendation: Verify that theme_block_asset('resources/IconSearch.svg') is correctly resolving the path. The $takt_current_block_folder global may not be set when this template renders. Consider inlining the SVG directly or using theme_block_asset_url() with an img tag as a fallback.

dropdownArrows.observations: The CSS fix (.archive select with white SVG background-image) has been applied in Archive.css lines 129-131. The select elements exist in the DOM across all archive sections with filters. In the 2x zoomed filter bar screenshots, the select elements ('Filter by Topic', 'Filter by Audience', 'Filter by Resource Type', per-page '9') render as pill-shaped controls on the dark background. The dropdown arrows are subtle white chevrons rendered via CSS background-image. At normal resolution they are small but present; at 2x zoom they become more visible. The Figma design shows a small down-chevron on 'FILTER BY TOPIC' and the per-page '9' select, which is consistent with what the CSS override provides. The per-page select width has been increased from w-16 to w-20, preventing the arrow from clipping the number text.

cardHoverStates.observations: Policy card hover state was captured successfully. The first policy card ('Academic Integrity Policy') shows a full lime/yellow (accent) background fill on hover, matching the Figma hover state (811:59964). Tag pill ('ACADEMIC INTEGRITY') inverts to a white/light background with border. Title text becomes dark charcoal. 'VIEW RESOURCE' CTA text becomes charcoal. The CSS in Archive.css confirms btn-tertiary overrides with !important rules (lines 144-163) that force charcoal color on group:hover for both resource and policy cards, preventing the dark-on-dark flash. Team member cards show lime accent background on hover with correct charcoal text inversion. The transition-colors CSS (lines 117-124) provides smooth color transitions for resource and policy cards.

typography.notes: No regression from revision 0. All typography matches Figma: Anton headings, body font for descriptions, text-body-small uppercase for labels and tag pills.

colors.notes: No regression. Dark charcoal backgrounds, white headings, off-white descriptions, accent-colored tag pills, category-specific accent colors on blog cards.

spacing.notes: No regression. Section padding, card grid gaps, card internal padding, filter bar gap all consistent with Figma within tolerance.

layout.notes: No regression. Desktop 4-column blog grid, 3-column resource/policy/team grid, tablet 2-column, mobile single column all correct. Search input now properly sized alongside filter dropdowns.

responsiveBehavior.notes: Mobile (375px): Search input and filter dropdowns go full-width (max-sm:w-full). Filter bar wraps naturally. Desktop (1440px): Search input sits inline with filter dropdown at matched width.

issues.figmaValue: Magnifying glass icon visible at right inside search input
issues.implementationValue: Submit button exists in DOM but contains no visible SVG icon
issues.recommendation: Verify theme_block_asset('resources/IconSearch.svg') resolves correctly. The IconSearch.svg file exists at blocks/Archive/resources/IconSearch.svg. The $takt_current_block_folder global may not be set during render. Consider inlining the SVG markup directly in Archive.php as a reliable fallback.

change.to: Inline the SVG content directly or debug theme_block_asset path resolution

notes.description: Firefox and WebKit browsers continue to fail screenshot capture (SSL cert issue with .localhost domain). Only Chromium screenshots were captured. This is an environment issue, not a code issue.

The search input now uses min-w-58 (232px) without grow-1, matching the approximate width of filter dropdowns. This is a visual improvement from the previous implementation where the search input stretched to fill available space.

The white SVG dropdown arrow override in Archive.css correctly addresses the dark mode context where the global forms.css currentColor approach fails. All select elements now show visible white chevron arrows.

The !important overrides for btn-tertiary in resource and policy card hover context (lines 144-163 in Archive.css) successfully prevent the dark-on-dark flash. The CTA text correctly transitions to charcoal on accent background hover. The underline suppression on hover is also appropriate since the entire card is the clickable element.
